channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Texans look for answers after embarrassing 31-2 home loss to the Ravens
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Quarterback C.J. Stroud summed up Houston’s performance in an embarrassing 31-2 loss to Baltimore on Wednesday. “Sometimes you go out there and lay an egg,” Stroud said. “It’s not easy to say that, but that’s what happened. Didn’t play well from the first play to the last.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bridges scores season-high 41, Knicks withstand Wembanyama's monster Christmas debut to edge Spurs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mikal Bridges scored a season-high 41 points, and the New York Knicks overcame 42 from Victor Wembanyama in a monster Christmas debut to edge the San Antonio Spurs 117-114 on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chiefs' Travis Kelce appears in 'Happy Gilmore 2' trailer
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs star tight end Travis Kelce appeared in the "Happy Gilmore 2" trailer, appearing to play the part of an employee at a golf course.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Cal QB Mendoza lands at Indiana in transfer portal: reports
item.description: Mendoza, one of the top quarterbacks available in the transfer portal, will join a team that made the College Football Playoff after a remarkable turnaround season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Nowels, The Mercury News

item.title: How name, image and likeness laws have changed college sports
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled in 2021 the NCAA could not prevent student athletes from profiting from their game. Since the decision, legal fights have continued between the NCAA and US states.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Players have mixed feelings about being on the road on Christmas as NFL adds more holiday games
item.description: OWINGS MILLS, Md. (AP) — Asked if there was any special meaning to playing on Christmas for a second straight season, Lamar Jackson paused for several seconds before answering. That said it all. “I mean, it is,” he finally replied, trying to be diplomatic.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shedeur Sanders chimes in as Colorado star Travis Hunter's relationship with fiancée faces mounting criticism
item.description: The Colorado Buffaloes quarterback came his teammate Travis Hunter's defense amid the drama surrounding the two-way star's relationship with Leanna Lenee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blackhawks drop 2nd straight on road trip in 4-3 loss to Wild
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Brock Faber snapped a tie in the third period, and the Minnesota Wild stopped a four-game losing streak by edging the Blackhawks, 4-3, on Monday night. Kirill Kaprizov, Jared Spurgeon and Marcus Foligno also scored for Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Bucks roll past Bulls, 112-91, despite being without 2 star players
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — No Dame, no Giannis, no problem for the Milwaukee Bucks on Monday night. The Bulls, on the other hand, had plenty of them.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: No. 14 Gonzaga's matchup with No. 22 UCLA is the headliner on the week's light AP Top 25 schedule
item.description: Gonzaga coach Mark Few hadn't been off the court long over the weekend when the focus turned to preparing for the 14th-ranked Bulldogs' matchup with a ranked opponent days after Christmas. The good news was he could start thinking about it after a win, unlike UCLA's Mick Cronin — whose No.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sabres end 13-game losing streak with 7-1 win over Islanders, look forward to continued success
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Buffalo Sabres can carry the good cheer of ending the longest losing streak in the NHL this season into their holiday break.
item.dc:creator: ALLAN KREDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Houston Texans wide receiver Tank Dell released from hospital after severe knee injury
item.description: It’s the second straight year Tank Dell has sustained what is almost certain to be a season-ending injury.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 49ers eliminated from NFC playoffs before game against Miami. Cowboys, Saints, Cardinals also out
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — The defending NFC champion San Francisco 49ers were eliminated from the playoffs before their game at Miami began on Sunday because of victories by Washington and the Los Angeles Rams.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: High-scoring Lions' fast start too much for Bears, Chicago loses 9th in a row
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bears spotted the Detroit Lions an early 20-0 lead and despite catching their groove midway through the game, Chicago came up short for the second time this season against Detroit.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Bears vs. Lions: Injuries, inactives and key matchups for Sunday's game at Soldier Field
item.description: UPDATE: The Bears and Lions have announced their inactives for Sunday's game. See INJURY REPORT below. CHICAGO — The Bears are "playing out the string," as they say, eliminated from NFL playoff contention with three games still left to play this season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: John Seidler takes over as control person of Padres after Peter's death
item.description: John Seidler, an entrepreneur and business executive, has been named the next control person of the San Diego Padres, pending approval by Major League Baseball, after the death of his brother Peter Seidler, the team's former owner.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Northwestern demolishes DePaul in crosstown showdown
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. (WGN) — An early blitz on the offensive end and playmaking down the stretch from Brooks Barnhizer and Nick Martinelli helped seal a convincing win for Northwestern over DePaul in the yearly crosstown showdown between the two programs.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, the league confirmed. The all-time MLB leader in stolen bases and one of the games greatest leadoff hitters died in Oakland on Friday after a bout with pneumonia, TMZ reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Mikheyev stars as 'Hawks beat Kraken 3-1 for 3rd straight win
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Ilya Mikheyev scored two more goals, Arvid Soderblom made 27 saves and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Seattle Kraken 3-1 on Thursday night for their third straight win. Teuvo Teravainen had two assists as Chicago closed out a perfect three-game homestand.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: San Diego FC releases 2025 regular season schedule
item.description: San Diego FC will be kicking off its first regular season match in franchise history in February against a rival city.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Bucs' OC Liam Coen addresses NFL head coaching rumors
item.description: Tampa Bay Buccaneers' first-year offensive coordinator Liam Coen has been excellent calling plays for the Bucs this season.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Real Salt Lake's 2025 MLS season kicks off Feb. 22 with away game in San Jose
item.description: Real Salt Lake unveiled its entire 2025 MLS Season on Thursday, along with a few crucial dates such as the club's return to the CONCACAF Champions Cup.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker said it is “debatable” whether transgender women will “always” have a physical advantage over cisgender, or non-transgender, women.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Utah HC erases two-goal deficit to stun Vancouver
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Mikhail Sergachev scored with 12 seconds left in overtime, as the Utah Hockey Club stormed back from a 2-0 deficit in the third period to beat the Vanouver Canucks and end a five-game home losing streak, 3-2.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
